pattern: To make a fixed-length string lexicographically smallest for a target total, keep every early letter at its minimum and dump all of the extra value onto the trailing letters.
hints: Every letter must be at least 'a', so start from a string of all 'a's and figure out how much value is still owed beyond that baseline.
To stay lexicographically smallest, the leftmost letters must stay as small as possible, so any extra value should be pushed as far right as it can go.
Walk backward from the last position, giving each position as much of the remaining owed value as it can hold (up to 'z', i.e. 25 above the 'a' baseline) before moving to the position before it.

edge_inputs.description: minimum possible k for length 1 gives 'a'
maximum possible k for length 1 gives 'z'
k equal to n: every letter is 'a'
k equal to 26 * n: every letter is 'z'
only 1 unit above the minimum: exactly the last letter bumps to 'b'
exactly two trailing letters must go above 'a' (owed value spills past one position)
owed value exactly fills one letter to 'z' with nothing left over

stress.description: random valid (n, k) pairs across the full length range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100000)))
    k = rng.randint(n, 26 * n)
    return [n, k]
stress.note: k is always drawn within the valid [n, 26n] range
